wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction & Design Principles
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the design system introduction and design principles section. Explain the project's purpose as a professional B2C design system focused on calm, intentional, and cohesive user interfaces. Document the design philosophy emphasizing consistency, accessibility, and scalability. Describe the target audience including frontend developers and product teams. Explain the relationship between design tokens, component architecture, and layout systems. Include the project's core values, design constraints, and architectural decisions. Provide context on why this design system exists and how it differs from traditional component libraries. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples demonstrating the design system's approach to solving common UI challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Base Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for base UI components that form the foundation of the design system. Document the Button component including all variants (primary, secondary, ghost, destructive), sizes (small, medium, large), disabled states, and accessibility features. Explain the Input component with form integration patterns, validation states, error handling, placeholder behavior, and keyboard navigation. Detail the prop interfaces defined in types/index.ts and how they ensure type safety across components. Include practical code examples showing component usage in forms, buttons with icons, and input validation scenarios. Document CSS custom property consumption from design tokens and how components maintain consistent styling. Address component composition patterns and integration with other UI elements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the design system. Include detailed installation instructions using npm/yarn/package managers. Document the development environment setup with Node.js requirements and dependency installation. Provide step-by-step quick start guide showing how to import and use core components in a React application. Include basic usage examples demonstrating Button, Input, and other fundamental components. Document the build process and available scripts for development, testing, and production builds. Explain the project structure and how to integrate the design system into existing applications. Include troubleshooting common setup issues and environment configuration. Provide code examples for both TypeScript and JavaScript usage patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for layout components that structure the overall application interface. Document the TopBar component including navigation elements, branding placement, status indicators, and responsive behavior. Explain the ContextHeader component for content hierarchy, breadcrumb navigation, and contextual information display. Detail the PrimaryWorkspace component as the main content container, documenting how it arranges child components and manages layout constraints. Include component composition patterns showing how these layout components work together to create cohesive page structures. Document prop interfaces for passing content and configuration to each layout component. Provide examples of common layout arrangements and how to customize spacing and alignment. Address responsive design considerations and accessibility features for screen readers and keyboard navigation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design System Foundation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the design system foundation covering design tokens, color palettes, typography, spacing, and layout constraints. Document the CSS custom property system and how design tokens are structured and consumed throughout components. Explain the color system including semantic meanings, accessibility contrast ratios, and brand customization options. Detail the typography scale with font families, sizes, weights, and line heights. Document the spacing system and grid relationships used for consistent layout. Cover the type system including TypeScript interfaces and type safety patterns. Explain how design tokens cascade through the component hierarchy and how to override defaults. Include guidelines for maintaining design token consistency and extending the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for content presentation components that display information and facilitate user interaction. Document the Card component including content containers, elevation effects, hover states, and interactive elements. Explain the SecondaryPanel component for contextual information display, side panels, and supplementary content areas. Detail component styling patterns, shadow systems, and spacing configurations using design tokens. Include examples of card layouts, panel positioning, and content organization patterns. Document component variants for different content types and interaction modes. Address responsive behavior and mobile-first design considerations. Explain integration patterns with other components and how content components handle dynamic content loading and state changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Library Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive component library documentation covering all UI components in the design system. Document the component architecture pattern and how components consume design tokens. Explain the component composition approach and prop interface patterns used throughout the library. Cover base components (Button, Input), layout components (TopBar, ContextHeader, PrimaryWorkspace), content components (Card), and utility components (StatusBadge, ProgressIndicator, SecondaryPanel, ProofFooter). For each component, document props interfaces, usage patterns, accessibility features, and customization options. Include code examples demonstrating common use cases and integration patterns. Explain component states, variants, and responsive behavior. Document the relationship between components and how they work together to create cohesive user interfaces.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for utility components that provide visual feedback and enhance user experience. Document the StatusBadge component including status indicators (not-started, in-progress, shipped), color semantics, and accessibility features for screen readers. Explain the ProgressIndicator component for step navigation, completion tracking, and user guidance. Detail the ProofFooter component for completion verification, summary displays, and finalization states. Include component prop interfaces, state management patterns, and integration with form workflows. Document visual design patterns, animation behaviors, and transition effects. Provide examples of common usage scenarios such as multi-step forms, status tracking dashboards, and completion screens. Address accessibility compliance including ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, and focus management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Usage Examples & Integration Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive usage examples and integration patterns documentation showcasing practical implementations of the design system. Document complete application examples demonstrating how to compose components into functional user interfaces. Explain integration patterns for forms, navigation, content layouts, and interactive workflows. Include real-world scenarios such as dashboard layouts, checkout flows, and admin interfaces. Document state management patterns and how components interact with application state. Provide examples of responsive design implementation and mobile-first considerations. Include best practices for component composition, prop drilling alternatives, and performance optimization. Demonstrate accessibility implementation patterns and inclusive design practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy & Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing strategy documentation covering the design system's quality assurance approach. Document the testing framework setup using Vitest and React Testing Library. Explain design token verification tests ensuring consistency across the system. Detail component testing approaches including unit tests, integration tests, and accessibility tests. Document layout testing strategies and responsive behavior validation. Include testing patterns for form components, state management, and user interactions. Explain snapshot testing for visual regression prevention. Document testing best practices for design system components and how to write effective tests for custom implementations. Include guidelines for maintaining test coverage and debugging test failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contributing & Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive contributing and development guidelines for the design system project. Document the development environment setup including Node.js requirements, dependency installation, and local development workflow. Explain the component creation process following established patterns and conventions. Detail design token maintenance procedures and how to add new tokens or modify existing ones. Document the pull request process, code review guidelines, and quality standards. Include component documentation requirements and testing obligations for new contributions. Explain the release process and versioning strategy. Document coding standards, naming conventions, and architectural principles contributors must follow. Provide troubleshooting guides for common development issues and environment setup problems.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Design System Project Overview

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
This project is a comprehensive design system implementation for building professional B2C products with a focus on calm, intentional, and cohesive user interfaces. The system serves as both a functional application showcase and a reusable component library.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Establish a consistent design language with limited color palette (4 colors maximum)
- Provide reusable React components with clear props interfaces
- Implement design tokens for maintainable theming
- Demonstrate practical usage through an interactive job notification app
- Support accessibility with proper ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation

### Target Audience
- Frontend developers building B2C applications
- Design teams implementing design systems
- Product teams requiring consistent UI components
- Developers seeking clean, accessible React component patterns

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The project follows a modular React architecture with clear separation between layout components, atomic UI elements, and supporting utilities.

```mermaid
graph TD
    App[App Component] --> DefaultLayout[DefaultLayout]
    DefaultLayout --> TopBar[TopBar]
    DefaultLayout --> ContextHeader[ContextHeader]
    DefaultLayout --> PrimaryWorkspace[PrimaryWorkspace]
    DefaultLayout --> SecondaryPanel[SecondaryPanel]
    DefaultLayout --> ProofFooter[ProofFooter]
    
    PrimaryWorkspace --> Card[Card]
    PrimaryWorkspace --> FormSection[Form Elements]
    FormSection --> Input[Input]
    FormSection --> Button[Button]
    
    Card --> Content[Component Showcase]
    Card --> FormDemo[Form Demo]
    Card --> ButtonDemo[Button Demo]
    Card --> StatusDemo[Status Demo]
    Card --> NavigationDemo[Navigation Demo]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Component Composition Pattern**: Layout components accept child components via props
- **Design Token Pattern**: Centralized CSS variables for consistent theming
- **Atomic Design Pattern**: Individual components (Button, Input) composed into larger structures
- **Props Interface Pattern**: Strong TypeScript typing for all component interfaces

### System Relationships
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[App.tsx] --> B[DefaultLayout.tsx]
    B --> C[TopBar.tsx]
    B --> D[ContextHeader.tsx]
    B --> E[PrimaryWorkspace.tsx]
    B --> F[SecondaryPanel.tsx]
    B --> G[ProofFooter.tsx]
    
    E --> H[Card.tsx]
    E --> I[Button.tsx]
    E --> J[Input.tsx]
    
    K[types/index.ts] --> A
    K --> B
    K --> C
    K --> D
    K --> E
    K --> F
    K --> G
    K --> H
    K --> I
    K --> J
    
    L[tokens.css] --> M[Component Styles]
    M --> I
    M --> J
    M --> H
    M --> N[Layout Styles]
    N --> B
```

### Data Flow
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant App
    participant State
    participant Components
    
    User->>App: Interact with UI
    App->>State: Update state values
    State->>Components: Trigger re-render
    Components->>User: Display updated UI
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Root**: `src/main.tsx` - React DOM rendering setup
- **Main Application**: `src/App.tsx` - Component composition and state management
- **Build Configuration**: `vite.config.ts` - Vite build system configuration

### Core Modules
- **Component Library**: `src/components/` - Reusable UI components (Button, Input, Card, etc.)
- **Layout System**: `src/layouts/` - Page structure components
- **Type Definitions**: `src/types/index.ts` - TypeScript interfaces for all components
- **Design Tokens**: `src/styles/tokens.css` - Centralized design system variables

### Configuration Approach
- **Package Management**: `package.json` - Development dependencies including React, TypeScript, and testing tools
- **Testing Setup**: `tests/setup.ts` - Test environment configuration
- **Build Tools**: Vite-based configuration for fast development and production builds

### External Dependencies
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[design-system] --> B[React 18+]
    A --> C[TypeScript ~5.9]
    A --> D[Vite 8.0+]
    A --> E[React Testing Library]
    A --> F[Vitest]
    
    B --> G[React DOM]
    B --> H[React Strict Mode]
```

### Integration Points
- **Component Styling**: CSS modules with design token imports
- **State Management**: React hooks for local component state
- **Accessibility**: Proper ARIA attributes and semantic HTML
- **Responsive Design**: Media queries for mobile adaptation

### Component Relationship Diagram
```mermaid
classDiagram
    class App {
        +useState()
        +render()
    }
    
    class DefaultLayout {
        +topBar: ReactNode
        +contextHeader: ReactNode
        +primaryWorkspace: ReactNode
        +secondaryPanel: ReactNode
        +proofFooter: ReactNode
    }
    
    class Button {
        +variant: primary|secondary
        +size: sm|md|lg
        +disabled: boolean
        +onClick(): void
    }
    
    class Input {
        +label: string
        +placeholder: string
        +value: string
        +onChange(): void
        +error: string
    }
    
    class Card {
        +children: ReactNode
    }
    
    App --> DefaultLayout
    DefaultLayout --> Button
    DefaultLayout --> Input
    DefaultLayout --> Card
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Interactive Component Showcase**: Demonstrates all design system components in action
- **Form Element Testing**: Shows input validation, error states, and accessibility features
- **Button Variants**: Multiple button styles with consistent sizing and interaction patterns
- **Status Management**: Visual indicators for different workflow states
- **Step Navigation**: Interactive progress tracking with state management

### Implementation Highlights
- **Design Token System**: Centralized theming through CSS custom properties
- **Accessible Form Components**: Proper labeling, error handling, and ARIA attributes
- **Responsive Layout**: Mobile-first design with flexible component arrangements
- **Type Safety**: Comprehensive TypeScript interfaces for all component interactions

### Feature Architecture
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> AppInitialization
    AppInitialization --> ComponentRendering
    ComponentRendering --> UserInteraction
    UserInteraction --> StateUpdate
    StateUpdate --> ComponentRendering
    UserInteraction --> FormValidation
    FormValidation --> ErrorDisplay
    ErrorDisplay --> ComponentRendering
    UserInteraction --> StatusChange
    StatusChange --> ComponentRendering
```

### Component Showcase Features
- **Button Variants**: Primary and secondary buttons with hover/focus states
- **Input States**: Standard, error, disabled, and focused states
- **Card Layouts**: Content containers with consistent spacing and borders
- **Progress Indicators**: Step navigation with state management
- **Status Badges**: Visual indicators for workflow progression

The design system demonstrates clean, maintainable React component architecture while providing a comprehensive foundation for building professional B2C applications with consistent design language and robust accessibility features.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [App.tsx](src/App.tsx)
- [DefaultLayout.tsx](src/layouts/DefaultLayout/DefaultLayout.tsx)
- [Button.tsx](src/components/Button/Button.tsx)
- [Input.tsx](src/components/Input/Input.tsx)
- [Card.tsx](src/components/Card/Card.tsx)
- [tokens.css](src/styles/tokens.css)
- [main.tsx](src/main.tsx)
- [index.ts](src/types/index.ts)
- [vite.config.ts](vite.config.ts)
- [setup.ts](tests/setup.ts)
- [Button.css](src/components/Button/Button.css)
- [Input.css](src/components/Input/Input.css)
- [DefaultLayout.css](src/layouts/DefaultLayout/DefaultLayout.css)
- [Card.css](src/components/Card/Card.css)

</blog>
